rationale: In the background section, the authors explicitly use Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) as evidence that online user-generated reviews can influence sales, not merely as a passing name. They then contrast that prior stream with their own gap statement that most studies examine one earned-media type in isolation. This citation helps frame the paper's core motivation for an integrated model of traditional and social earned media effects.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Chevalier and Mayzlin's WOM-sales evidence by moving from a single online review context to a joint analysis of traditional and social earned media and their interdependence. It extends the line of inquiry to a different setting and broader media system perspective.
